cards.back:
to hold firmly; to understand
Ex: She grasped the concept quickly.
cards.correct_answer_hint: Grasp means to hold firmly or to understand something

cards.back:
so slight as to be hard to notice
Ex: There was a subtle difference in tone.
cards.correct_answer_hint: Subtle means something so slight it is difficult to notice

cards.back:
to continue doing something despite difficulty
Ex: He persisted despite the obstacles.
cards.correct_answer_hint: Persist means to keep going even when things are difficult

cards.back:
to use something to maximum advantage
Ex: She leveraged her contacts to land the job.
cards.correct_answer_hint: Leverage means to use something to your maximum advantage

cards.back:
based on random choice rather than reason
Ex: The rule seemed completely arbitrary.
cards.correct_answer_hint: Arbitrary means based on random choice with no clear reason

cards.back:
logical and consistent; easy to understand
Ex: His argument was coherent and well-structured.
cards.correct_answer_hint: Coherent means logical and consistent, easy to follow

cards.back:
having more than one possible meaning
Ex: His answer was deliberately ambiguous.
cards.correct_answer_hint: Ambiguous means something that can be interpreted in more than one way

cards.back:
giving a lot of information clearly in a few words
Ex: Write a concise summary.
cards.correct_answer_hint: Concise means expressing a lot clearly with few words

cards.back:
implied but not directly expressed
Ex: There was an implicit agreement between them.
cards.correct_answer_hint: Implicit means something that is understood but not directly stated

cards.back:
the level at which something begins or changes
Ex: We crossed the pain threshold.
cards.correct_answer_hint: A threshold is the point at which something begins or changes
